methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by JB Straubel, currently valued at $6.0B post-money after raising $1.0B+. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable set of 9 private frontier-tech peers and 1 public comparable (Eos Energy Enterprises, LNZA), spanning battery recycling, hydrogen, fusion, and long-duration energy storage.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $6.0B Series D post-money valuation, applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D to later-stage progression, implying a forward valuation of $9.0B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals at $2.0B (Series C), Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B (Series C), and Amogy at $700M (Series C). The median of this set is $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B, implying a valuation of $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods produces a range of $850M (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.0B. The substantial spread reflects tension between the company's current post-money valuation and stage-typical peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the maturity profile of Redwood's battery recycling operations relative to earlier-stage fusion and hydrogen peers in the comp set, and the applicability of public comparable multiples to the private frontier-tech cohort.
